text.title: Stack-to-gate matrix
text.summary: The matrix maps which test gates are mandatory based on what the feature actually touches. The git diff is the input; the matrix is the lookup.

list.item: Backend only → unit tests REQUIRED + API tests REQUIRED. No Playwright. No UI audit.
Backend + Frontend (full-stack feature) → unit tests REQUIRED + API tests REQUIRED + Playwright happy path REQUIRED. UI audit REQUIRED if any visible UI changed.
User-facing flow (any surface where the user takes ordered actions) → Playwright positive AND negative cases REQUIRED per flow. UI audit REQUIRED if web/mobile/TUI.
Frontend only (no backend changes) → unit tests REQUIRED + Playwright pos+neg per flow REQUIRED. No API tests for this feature.
Pure data (migration, ETL, no UI / API contract change) → unit tests REQUIRED + migration tests REQUIRED + reversibility check REQUIRED. No Playwright. No UI audit. No API tests.
Infrastructure / CI / build-system → unit tests on any logic changed + smoke check that build / deploy still produces a working artefact.

rule.statement: Applicability is computed from the git diff, NOT from the developer's self-assessment of scope.
rule.rationale: Authors systematically underestimate which layers their change touches. The diff is the source of truth; the matrix is mechanical.

antipattern.description: Claiming "no UI change" because the developer thinks the visible diff is cosmetic.
antipattern.reason: Cosmetic changes still touch UI; the matrix says UI audit is required if any visible element changes. The escape hatch is "noted, no behaviour change" with one line — not silent omission.
